mnemonics.title: 2PL Phases
mnemonics.content: GROWS: Growing Requires Only Wait, Shrinking Starts releasing - G-R-O-W-S helps remember Growing phase is for Obtaining locks, Shrinking is for Releasing locks

mnemonics.title: Deadlock Conditions
mnemonics.content: M-H-N-C: Mutual exclusion, Hold and wait, No preemption, Circular wait - Remember as 'My Hat Never Comes'

mnemonics.title: Lock Types
mnemonics.content: S-SHARED = See (read only), X-XCLUSIVE = X-change (write) - S allows 'See', X allows 'Change'

mnemonics.title: Strict vs Rigorous
mnemonics.content: Strict = S-only until end, Rigorous = All (S+X) until end - 'Strict waits for X, Rigorous waits for both'

acronyms.usage: A concurrency control protocol with growing and shrinking phases
Holds X-locks until commit to prevent cascading rollbacks
Holds all locks until commit, stricter than 2PL-S
Acquires all locks before execution to prevent deadlocks
Allows reading but not writing, multiple S-locks compatible
Allows both reading and writing, incompatible with all other locks
Software system managing concurrent database access
Alternative concurrency control using timestamps instead of locks

visualTips.visualization: Draw a timeline: Growing phase = upward slope (locks increase), Lock point = peak, Shrinking phase = downward slope (locks decrease)
Create a 2x2 matrix: Rows = requested lock, Columns = held lock. Put 'Yes' where compatible, 'No' where not. S-S = Yes, S-X = No, X-S = No, X-X = No
Draw a circle with T1→T2→T3→T1 showing circular wait condition
Show multiple transactions as horizontal bars, mark where each reaches max locks - that point determines serializability order
Use a bar chart: Height = number of locks held. Grows upward during growing phase, shrinks downward during shrinking phase

analogies.analogy: Like shopping with a shopping cart - you can keep adding items (locks) to your cart, but you can't checkout (release) until you're done picking everything
Like leaving a restaurant - once you start leaving (releasing locks), you can't go back to get more food (acquire new locks)
Multiple people reading a book at the same time - everyone can 'read' the content without changing it
Only one person can have the only pen to write in a shared notebook - they have complete control
Two people each holding one item and waiting for the other's item - like两人互相拿着对方的伞等对方先放手
One person always being skipped in a queue while others keep getting served - they never get their turn
Like keeping your library book until you return it - you can't let others borrow even what you've only read yet
Making a complete shopping list before going to the store and buying everything at once, rather than making multiple trips
